title: Manual picking and passaging of hPSC colonies
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"edj92","text":"This protocol describes picking and expansion of many colonies at once (manual clump passaging) and the picking of single colony (positive selection).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"cuq7","text":"Depending on the growth characteristics of specific hPSC line, cells must be passaged every 5-7 days in\norder to maintain log phase of growth and avoid induction of differentiation. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3e8h1","text":"Recommended split ratio for established cultures is around 5 colonies per 10 cm2 surface area (i.e. 5 colonies per well of a 6-well plate (or 35 mm dish) or 10 colonies per 60 mm dish.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":79,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"7nmtq","text":"Manual clump passaging is recommended for colonies grown on vitronectin* or on feeder cells since their surface adhesion is less strong than when cultured on matrigel, geltrex or laminin. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2bf39","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Using a syringe with a needle or a stem cell knife cut about 5 colonies/10cm 2 surface area into small pieces ( i.e. 5 colonies per well of a 6-well plate (or 35 mm dish) or 10 colonies per 60 mm dish ). Figure 1 . Schematic of the mechanical cutting of a colony from IMAGINi004-A iPSC line
Use a 200µL pipette with volume adjusted to 100µL to gently dislodge the pieces of colonies with the liquid stream from the pipette.
Add the appropriate volume of hPSC medium, refer to Table 1 for recommended volumes.

Visually inspect the quality of the hiPSC culture under a stereomicroscope. For assesment of culture quality see Reference pictures of hiPSC cultured in defined conditions . If needed remove the differentiated regions as described in protocol: Maintenance of hPSC
Prepare vitronectin-coated vessels according to how many clones are to be picked, refer to protocol Coating of tissue Culture Vessels for hPSC

Pre-warm the required volume medium (refer to Table 1 ) at Room temperature for 00:30:00 in 50 mL sterile Falcon tubes. A B C D 1 Culture Vessel Surface Area [cm2]* Total volume [ml] 2 6 well plate 1 well ~9,6 2 3 35 mm dish 1 dish ~9.6 2 4 60 mm dish 1 dish ~20 4 5 12 well plate 1 well ~4 1 6 24 well plate 1 well ~2 0,5 7 24 well plate 24 wells 12 Table 1. volume of medium to be prepared according to culture format
Aspirate the medium containing floating and/or differentiated cells.
Remove the culture vessel containing the cells from the incubator.
Transfer the medium containing the pieces of colonies to the destination culture vessel using 1000µL pipette with volume adjusted to 1000µL.
Aspirate the residual coating solution from the destination culture vessel.
Incubate the vessels at 37 °C and at 5 % volume CO 2 .
If necessary to repeat, add additional media to the source culture vessel and repeat from step 7 to 14 as many times as necessary, depending on the number of new dishes required.
If needed adjust the volume of medium in the destination dish to the total volume. Refer to Table 1 for recommended volumes.
Remove the culture vessel containing the cells from the incubator.
Aspirate the medium containing floating and/or differentiated cells.
Add the appropriate volume of hPSC medium, refer to Table 2 for recommended volumes.
Using a syringe with a needle or a stem cell knife cut out the desired colony.
Use a 200µL pipette with volume adjusted to 100µL to gently dislodge the pieces of colonies with the liquid stream from the pipette.
Immediately transfer the pieces of colony with the same 200µL pipette.
Aspirate the residual coating solution from the destination culture vessel. Cells grown on vitronectin can be transferred onto any other matrix.
If needed adjust the volume of medium in the destination dish to the toal volume, refer to Table 2 for recommended volumes.
Change the needle of the syringe and repeat from step 6 to 13 as many times as necessary, depending on the number of colonies that have to be picked.
Incubate the vessels at 37 °C and at 5 % volume CO 2 .

Pre-warm the required volume medium (refer to Table 2 ) at Room temperature for 00:30:00 in 50 mL sterile Falcon tubes. A B C D 1 Culture Vessel Surface Area [cm2]* Total volume [ml] 2 35 mm dish 1 dish ~9.6 2 3 12 well plate 1 well ~4 1 4 24 well plate 1 well ~2 0,5 5 24 well plate 24 wells 12 Table 2. Volume of medium to be prepared according to vessel format.
